title: prunus triloba f. multiplex
intro: Prunus triloba f. multiplex, also known as the Korean cherry or trilobed cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its multiple branches and clusters of white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Prunus triloba f. multiplex include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Prunus triloba f. multiplex typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
